aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the collision of sanctity, lust, labor, rot, and family resentment. In edge cases, choose the more concrete, uncomfortable, and character-specific rendering over symbolic safety; if an image or action feels too polite for the pressure, it is probably wrong.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, violence, shame, grief, or economic pressure drives a scene, vague language counts as a failed beat; replace euphemism with the exact bodily, social, or material consequence the character experiences.

chapter_spine.conflict: Returned drowned, rising monsoon water, and the abrupt reunion of five estranged siblings at Sereya's abandoned ferry house.
chapter_spine.consequence: The siblings reunite and reluctantly accept that the pilgrimage channels must be reopened in lover-order or the dead will keep coming back upstream.
must_land_beats: A drowned girl returns to the ferry house with a salt-fig seed in her mouth.
Riva arrives expecting superstition and finds the family crisis materially undeniable.
Tovin names the five lovers whose channels must be reopened in sequence.
A second corpse and a sprouting seed force the siblings into reluctant alliance.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Returned drowned, rising monsoon water, and the abrupt reunion of five estranged siblings at Sereya's abandoned ferry house.
chapter_spine.state_shift: The siblings reunite and reluctantly accept that the pilgrimage channels must be reopened in lover-order or the dead will keep coming back upstream.

chapter_spine.conflict: Old shame inside the House of Oaths, the missing ledger page, and the drowned speaking in familiar voices.
chapter_spine.consequence: The mission becomes psychologically personalized when each sibling receives a private sign from the river and the torn ledger page turns into a live wound.
must_land_beats: The siblings sort Sereya's relics and immediately fall back into childhood hierarchy.
The reed ledger appears with a missing page near the final channel.
Paternity resentment and blame flare in the same room as devotional objects.
At night the drowned man's voice calls each sibling by name in a different register.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Old shame inside the House of Oaths, the missing ledger page, and the drowned speaking in familiar voices.
chapter_spine.state_shift: The mission becomes psychologically personalized when each sibling receives a private sign from the river and the torn ledger page turns into a live wound.

chapter_spine.conflict: Tovin's insistence that Reed Gate will not move without physical work and emotional truth.
chapter_spine.consequence: Reed Gate opens and Riva emerges as the practical center of the mission, giving the family proof that restoration is possible while breeding resentment.
must_land_beats: The siblings clear the first gate by hand under monsoon pressure.
Each child must name something Sereya took from them before the sluice will answer.
Tovin states plainly that Sereya never meant the route to belong to one heir.
The current finally catches the corpse-ferry and carries it downstream.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Tovin's insistence that Reed Gate will not move without physical work and emotional truth.
chapter_spine.state_shift: Reed Gate opens and Riva emerges as the practical center of the mission, giving the family proof that restoration is possible while breeding resentment.

chapter_spine.conflict: A temporary lull in danger that lets tenderness and suspicion re-emerge together.
chapter_spine.consequence: The siblings briefly recover a domestic baseline, then lose it when the missing ledger page and Daro's river-hearing reopen distrust.
must_land_beats: The family shares an unstrategic meal at Tovin's house.
Humor and remembered appetite establish what ordinary closeness used to feel like.
Lale notices the torn binding and realizes the missing page was deliberately removed.
Riva's suspicion falls toward Iven just as Daro hears singing under the floorboards.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: A temporary lull in danger that lets tenderness and suspicion re-emerge together.
chapter_spine.state_shift: The siblings briefly recover a domestic baseline, then lose it when the missing ledger page and Daro's river-hearing reopen distrust.

chapter_spine.conflict: Ilyan's shrine offers Daro paternity, public holiness, and the possibility of singular inheritance.
chapter_spine.consequence: Daro learns Ilyan is his father and begins to believe the river is naming him as Sereya's heir.
must_land_beats: Ilyan greets Daro as a son before explaining the second rite.
The siblings must speak aloud how Sereya used desire as leverage rather than blessing.
Pilgrims overhear the family's humiliating truths.
Daro sings Hymn Cut open and feels public recognition answer him.
Riva recognizes that Daro has found a form of authority she cannot simply outwork.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Ilyan's shrine offers Daro paternity, public holiness, and the possibility of singular inheritance.
chapter_spine.state_shift: Daro learns Ilyan is his father and begins to believe the river is naming him as Sereya's heir.

chapter_spine.conflict: Iven's hunger for money and speed colliding with ritual order, public spectacle, and unstable currents.
chapter_spine.consequence: A grotesque corpse-jam at Marrow Market turns the siblings' labor into a public emergency and strips Riva of uncontested authority.
must_land_beats: Iven monetizes the family's first success by selling passage promises and drawing a crowd.
Because the earlier cut has not settled, corpses and market traffic jam together in public view.
The comedy curdles into shame and real danger.
Town crews refuse easy help and Iven takes on debt trying to buy back supplies or goodwill.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Iven's hunger for money and speed colliding with ritual order, public spectacle, and unstable currents.
chapter_spine.state_shift: A grotesque corpse-jam at Marrow Market turns the siblings' labor into a public emergency and strips Riva of uncontested authority.

chapter_spine.conflict: Paska's tactile mapmaking, Lale's damaged memory, and the implication that the missing page hid a family catastrophe.
chapter_spine.consequence: Lale remembers tearing out the ledger page after Sereya's death and realizes their history contains an erased disaster called the sixth mouth.
must_land_beats: Paska refuses route help until Lale submits to new tide marks on her skin.
The tattoo session restores bodily memory instead of abstract recall.
Paska explains why Sereya distributed the route across lovers rather than heirs.
Lale remembers removing the page herself after reading the words 'sixth mouth'.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Paska's tactile mapmaking, Lale's damaged memory, and the implication that the missing page hid a family catastrophe.
chapter_spine.state_shift: Lale remembers tearing out the ledger page after Sereya's death and realizes their history contains an erased disaster called the sixth mouth.

chapter_spine.conflict: Sleepwalking townspeople, infected floodwater, Nami's injury, and a half-open third channel.
chapter_spine.consequence: The threat reaches the living, Nami is wounded and feverish, and delay becomes lethal rather than merely reputational.
must_land_beats: Living townspeople begin walking toward water under the seeds' influence.
Riva must split attention between the route and rescue.
Daro's gift is used instrumentally, worsening the hidden power struggle.
Nami sees a drowned infant in fever and the sixth-mouth mystery gains terrible shape.
The siblings keep moving even though Nami should rest.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Sleepwalking townspeople, infected floodwater, Nami's injury, and a half-open third channel.
chapter_spine.state_shift: The threat reaches the living, Nami is wounded and feverish, and delay becomes lethal rather than merely reputational.

chapter_spine.conflict: Brief safety at Lantern Quay, accumulated shame, and the knowledge that the missing page will hurt when recovered.
chapter_spine.consequence: Iven retrieves the missing page, admits his part in losing it, and the siblings briefly re-form as a family before the next rupture.
must_land_beats: Lantern Quay provides a true quiet chapter with food, gossip, and temporary ease.
The siblings speak about childhood, sex, and appetite rather than only the emergency.
Iven sleeps with Sena and the intimacy changes what he can admit aloud.
Sena returns the missing page and forces Iven to say Jun's name.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Brief safety at Lantern Quay, accumulated shame, and the knowledge that the missing page will hurt when recovered.
chapter_spine.state_shift: Iven retrieves the missing page, admits his part in losing it, and the siblings briefly re-form as a family before the next rupture.

chapter_spine.conflict: The restored ledger page, Jun's drowned absence, and the need to reinterpret Sereya without stopping the mission.
chapter_spine.consequence: The siblings learn Sereya and Bato lost a sixth child in a failed final rite, collapsing the saintly version of their mother.
must_land_beats: The page names Jun and ties him to a failed opening of Storm Mouth.
Tovin confirms that Sereya scattered the surviving children after Jun's death.
Each sibling reads the revelation differently, deepening rather than solving conflict.
The family continues only because stopping would abandon the living.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: The restored ledger page, Jun's drowned absence, and the need to reinterpret Sereya without stopping the mission.
chapter_spine.state_shift: The siblings learn Sereya and Bato lost a sixth child in a failed final rite, collapsing the saintly version of their mother.

chapter_spine.conflict: Enver's contempt, Iven's paternal shame, and the practical need for rope and fuel at Coil Reach.
chapter_spine.consequence: The ropehouse fire destroys Riva's dredge and forces Iven to accept a father he hates, leaving the mission materially crippled.
must_land_beats: Enver names Iven his son in public and refuses saint-family romance.
Father and son fight while trying to handle the fourth cut.
A dropped lamp ignites the ropehouse and escalates emotional damage into material loss.
Riva loses the Bitter Thread and most of the heavy gear.
The family is forced onto a smaller, harsher mode of travel.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Enver's contempt, Iven's paternal shame, and the practical need for rope and fuel at Coil Reach.
chapter_spine.state_shift: The ropehouse fire destroys Riva's dredge and forces Iven to accept a father he hates, leaving the mission materially crippled.

chapter_spine.conflict: Nami's fever, physical exhaustion, and the possibility that the journey is now impossible.
chapter_spine.consequence: The siblings choose to continue on Sereya's old hand-poled funeral ferry, bound by care and shared damage rather than confidence.
must_land_beats: The chapter centers bodily care rather than tactical progress.
Riva and Lale finally speak honestly about leaving Nami behind after Sereya's death.
Daro admits he has heard the drowned since childhood.
The family chooses to continue without the dredge, accepting slower and riskier travel.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Nami's fever, physical exhaustion, and the possibility that the journey is now impossible.
chapter_spine.state_shift: The siblings choose to continue on Sereya's old hand-poled funeral ferry, bound by care and shared damage rather than confidence.

chapter_spine.conflict: Sleepwalkers in Halcote, split responsibilities, and the need to win practical trust from inland towns.
chapter_spine.consequence: Lale proves she can lead outside the family, and inland communities begin treating the siblings as necessary rather than cursed.
must_land_beats: The siblings split their labor for the first time, with Lale leading inland containment.
Families resist admitting their own kin are entranced until they see it firsthand.
Lale uses Paska's tide logic and her own nerve to redirect walkers into shelter.
She makes a bodily choice she cannot narrate away when she strikes a man to keep him from the water.
Halcote lends real help to the final push.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Sleepwalkers in Halcote, split responsibilities, and the need to win practical trust from inland towns.
chapter_spine.state_shift: Lale proves she can lead outside the family, and inland communities begin treating the siblings as necessary rather than cursed.

chapter_spine.conflict: The final sea-cut, Bato's survival, and Nami's hunger to be chosen.
chapter_spine.consequence: Bato reveals that singular sainthood is a lie, but Daro steals Sereya's pole and commits himself to that lie anyway.
must_land_beats: The ruined chapel produces a living ghost when Bato appears alive.
Bato recounts the failed rite without sanctifying Sereya's motives.
Nami nearly volunteers herself because chosenness still feels cleaner than shared duty.
Daro steals the saint's pole and turns private belief into active schism.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: The final sea-cut, Bato's survival, and Nami's hunger to be chosen.
chapter_spine.state_shift: Bato reveals that singular sainthood is a lie, but Daro steals Sereya's pole and commits himself to that lie anyway.

chapter_spine.conflict: Daro's public inheritance claim, communal panic, and the flood crest arriving too quickly for nuance.
chapter_spine.consequence: The family splits into rival plans for the final rite, making a collision between private grief and public need unavoidable.
must_land_beats: Daro offers frightened people one body and one answer.
Riva fails to make collective labor sound glamorous enough for the desperate.
The conflict becomes public doctrine rather than private family argument.
The flood crest is fixed as an immediate deadline.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Daro's public inheritance claim, communal panic, and the flood crest arriving too quickly for nuance.
chapter_spine.state_shift: The family splits into rival plans for the final rite, making a collision between private grief and public need unavoidable.

chapter_spine.conflict: The monsoon crest, unstable channels, Daro's premature rite, and the siblings' already damaged bodies.
chapter_spine.consequence: Iven is maimed, pilgrims begin to drown in real time, and the siblings realize the last cut can only be opened if all five of them act together.
must_land_beats: The pursuit moves through breaking reed islands rather than clean action beats.
Nami nearly follows the dead overboard and has to be physically reclaimed.
Iven sacrifices his hand to swing Riva's ferry into position.
Daro opens Storm Mouth too early and turns ritual into suction catastrophe.
Tovin's key and Bato's warning finally clarify the collective form of the ending.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: The monsoon crest, unstable channels, Daro's premature rite, and the siblings' already damaged bodies.
chapter_spine.state_shift: Iven is maimed, pilgrims begin to drown in real time, and the siblings realize the last cut can only be opened if all five of them act together.

chapter_spine.conflict: The sea breach, gathered dead, Jun's memory, and the requirement that each sibling surrender private claims to chosenness.
chapter_spine.consequence: Storm Mouth opens under collective truth-telling, the dead finally move seaward, and Daro loses his singing voice instead of his life.
must_land_beats: Riva interrupts Daro's solo rite even though it risks killing him.
Each sibling gives up a lover-token and names an ugly truth Sereya left in them.
The ritual is powered by humiliation, grief, labor, and sequence rather than purity.
Daro redirects the song and destroys his own voice.
The dead finally take the sea road and the seeds go slack.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: The sea breach, gathered dead, Jun's memory, and the requirement that each sibling surrender private claims to chosenness.
chapter_spine.state_shift: Storm Mouth opens under collective truth-telling, the dead finally move seaward, and Daro loses his singing voice instead of his life.

chapter_spine.conflict: Cleanup, debt, injury, and the temptation to turn survival into a new saint legend.
chapter_spine.consequence: The siblings rebuild the ferry house as a shared crossing and accept lasting losses without returning to singular inheritance.
must_land_beats: The flood damage remains concrete: debt, ruined structures, altered bodies, and exhausted towns.
No one is allowed to turn the ending into another saint cult.
Riva decides to stay, Lale maps, Iven apprentices one-handed, Nami plants clean seed, and Daro keeps time by drum.
The book closes on a communal meal that feels lived rather than symbolic.
chapter_spine.pressure_source: Cleanup, debt, injury, and the temptation to turn survival into a new saint legend.
chapter_spine.state_shift: The siblings rebuild the ferry house as a shared crossing and accept lasting losses without returning to singular inheritance.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the norm across dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for ritual speech, emphasis, or deliberate character-specific formality.

premise: When the monsoon river begins returning the drowned with seeds in their mouths, the five estranged children of a ferryman-saint are forced back into the reed delta their mother once bound together by oath and lust. To keep the inland towns from following the dead underwater, they must reopen the half-forgotten pilgrimage channels in the order of her old lovers, each sibling becoming convinced the river has chosen them alone.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative movement aggressively: follow ordeal with meal, revelation with labor, schism with care, and public spectacle with close-bodied aftermath so no adjacent chapters share the same structural rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, bodily, locally sacred, and unsanitized; lyrical only when pressure earns it, never as a decorative default.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person rotating among the five siblings; each scene stays inside one consciousness and lets the river press through perception rather than omniscient explanation.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternate muscular propulsion with denser lyric accumulation; peak scenes should tighten and clarify rather than dissolve into fragmentary haze.
